name: Void-Blade Zealot
physical: Lithe, impossibly graceful warriors in form-fitting wraith-bone armor flowing like a second skin of living crystal. Armor shifts color — cyan at rest, violet in combat. Featureless visors glow with psychic emanations. No two identical — each suit grown organically over decades. No visible weapons. Hard-light blades MANIFEST from forearm crystalline nodes — one meter of shimmering razor-edged psychic energy. Cut at molecular level via atomic bond disruption.
narrative: What happens when a species mastered psychic combat for ten thousand years. They DANCE. Precognitive instinct choreographs every step. Phase Blinking through gunfire. Cuts so precise enemies take two steps after being struck before registering a severed spine. Against heavy armor: carve like scalpel through paper. Against shields: phase through as if nonexistent. The Shards mourn each Zealot — a century of training erased.

name: Prism-Sniper
physical: Slender figures in hooded wraith-bone cloaks shifting to match ambient light — nearly invisible stationary. Crystalline focusing array — meter-long prism aimed through psychic targeting interface. Fires needle-thin prismatic lance. Zero muzzle flash, zero sound. Visible for a fraction of a second as refracted color. Only sound: faint crystalline PING.
narrative: Does not miss. Psychic targeting accounts for wind, distance, movement, and the target's FUTURE position. First indication: a soldier falling dead with a perfectly circular hole through the helmet. Critical role: eliminate enemy Commanders from 500 meters. Energy damage is ineffective vs Guardians — switches to Shuriken (physical crystal darts) at endgame.

name: Grav-Tank
physical: Sleek angular craft hovering one meter on anti-gravity. No treads, no wheels. Wraith-bone hull — smooth, iridescent, seamless. Twin plasma lances project SUSTAINED beams boring through armor over 2–3 seconds. Target doesn't explode — it MELTS.
narrative: Sustained energy application — no armor thickness matters given time. Limitation: must hold position for 2–3 sec during fire; escorts essential. Hover crosses rivers, ravines. Can ascend cliff faces — if the enemy hides behind a wall, float over it.

name: Wraith-Construct
physical: Six-meter wraith-bone construct. Pilot psychically MERGED — consciousness extending through the entire frame. Humanoid but elongated. Fingers EXTEND into crystalline talons. Chest resonance chamber broadcasts Phase Blink +2% aura within 30m.
narrative: More than a vehicle — a union. Pilot surrenders individuality. When it falls: consciousness SNAPS back, traumatic separation. If the chamber is breached: consciousness may persist as echo for seconds — sometimes captured by a soul-stone. Sometimes dissipates. Gone.

name: Avatar of the Ancients
physical: Fifteen meters tall. Inhabited by a CHORUS of hundreds of ancient warrior consciousnesses. Wailing Doom sword — six meters of white-hot psychic fire carving reality. Eye-lances. Ground cracks from psychic pressure. Silence bubble extends 20 meters.
narrative: Physical manifestation of collective psychic legacy. 50m Phase Blink aura. The chorus SINGS — subsonic frequency felt as bone-deep resonance. When it falls: not a death rattle but a lament. Every Shard in earshot hears it. A piece of the species dies.

name: Aero-Fighter
physical: Crystalline grav-craft. No engine. Moves by psychic impulse — dimensional micro-shifts appearing as stuttering teleportation. Wingtip psychic energy lances with precognitive tracking.
narrative: Conventional targeting cannot predict where a teleporting craft will be. The Shards achieve air superiority through pure unpredictability — conventional lock-on systems register them as sensor ghosts.

name: Night-Weaver Bomber
physical: Disc-shaped, bends light around hull — functionally invisible to optical and most sensor systems. Payload: Containment Spheres — compressed dimensional energy that CRUSHES targets in localized reality distortions. Psychic targeting: pilot FEELS the ground through the hull.
narrative: Invisible death. Each sphere placed with surgical precision. The enemy sees nothing, then the ground implodes under them. No warning. No counterplay. Just aftermath.

name: Mirage-Caster
physical: Hovering crystalline arrays broadcasting psychic interference. Enemies within 200m: −20% accuracy, −15% movement speed, double-vision overlay, false radar returns, unshakable dread response.
narrative: Degrades combat effectiveness without dealing direct damage. Crews near a Caster fight BADLY — missing shots they should not miss, flinching at phantoms that aren't there. It doesn't kill the enemy. It makes them useless.

name: Sky-Chariot
physical: Open-topped grav-platform with psychic barrier fielding. Fast, exposed. Kinetic impact can breach the field and spill passengers mid-flight. Fastest faction transport.
narrative: Speed or safety. The Shards choose speed. Arrives seconds after launch — but one railgun hit can drop everyone. The cost of choosing extinction-level urgency over caution.

name: Storm-Bringer
physical: Central Distortion Cannon tears spacetime — gravity wells crush targets beneath multiplied weight. 4–5 sec per pulse, 3 pulses per recharge cycle. Buildings collapse. Vehicles crumple. Infantry driven through the ground.
narrative: Hovers above fortifications and CRUSHES them with multiplied gravity. Targets squeezed flat by invisible force. No blast, no fire — just inexorable compression. Fortification engineers call it the worst weapon in the galaxy.

name: Aether-Skiff
physical: Single psychic pilot IS the sensor array — FEELS other ships through the psychic medium at ranges exceeding conventional electronics. Fires Shuriken — physical crystal darts, bypassing energy shields entirely for anti-Guardian kinetic damage.
narrative: The critical kinetic-damage option against Guardians who are healed by energy weapons. The scout that can fight the endgame. Small, fast, fragile — but a Shuriken through a Guardian's containment field ends the engagement.

name: Prism-Frigate
physical: Scaled-up focusing array. Sustained laser beam cuts through capital armor at medium range over 3–4 seconds of continuous contact. Hull is light — not built for broadside exchanges. Must remain stationary during sustained fire cycle.
narrative: Fight in pairs: one fires, one screens. Patient, precise, devastating given time. A Prism-Frigate with a dedicated escort will eventually cut through anything. Without the escort, it is a stationary target.

name: Void-Stalker Cruiser
physical: Organic wraith-bone hull. Signature ability: Dimensional Shift — phases ENTIRE SHIP out of reality for 3 seconds. Dodges MAC rounds, torpedoes, boarding actions. Fires Ghostfire Torpedoes that phase through conventional shields before detonating inside the target hull.
narrative: The only capital ship that dodges killing blows by ceasing to exist. Fights on a different plane of reality. Veterans who have fought one describe the moment it vanishes under sustained fire as profoundly unsettling — like punching a ghost.

name: Celestial-Anchor
physical: Carrier plus psychic amplifier. Provides fleet-wide +5% Phase Blink chance while operational. Soul-stone recovery arrays capture the consciousness of destroyed units within 500m.
narrative: The fleet's heart. Destroying it removes the fleet-wide Phase Blink bonus immediately — the highest-priority target in any Shards engagement. Losses near it may be recovered via soul-stone. It is both command post and cemetery.

name: Infinity-Mothership
physical: Wraith-bone cathedral, small-moon-sized. 200-crew mental energy channeled into the Infinity Lance — a reality-warping beam. Relatively fragile hull for its size. Personal Phase Blink 18%. Charge time 15 seconds. One shot bisects a capital ship.
narrative: Overwhelming power in a beautiful, irreplaceable vessel. Losing it is a cultural extinction event — not just a tactical setback. The Infinity Lance is not a weapon. It is a monument to everything the Shards once were, turned against a galaxy that will not let them survive.
